text:
There was a small island off the coast of France. It was lush green, dotted with bright flowers, hidden away from the world. Every spring it bloomed beautifully, attracting butterflies.

On this island lived a retired teacher named Henri. Henri cherished his solitude, spending his days wandering its winding trails and picking wildflowers. One particular afternoon, after gathering an assortment of colorful blooms, he stopped under the shades of ancient olive trees to rest.

Suddenly, he heard a quiet voice.

"Good afternoon."

Henri looked up. There sat an old lady sitting on a rock, her knitting needles stuck in a ball of wool.

"Mon Dieu!" he exclaimed. "How did you find your way here?"

"Didn't say," she replied slyly. "But what I did say is that it looked like you needed a companion."

Henri chuckled. "How did you know?"

"Oh, I've noticed your picking. You prefer the ones with purple petals."

Henri laughed. "
